rationale: In the price-elasticity section, the citing paper discusses Van Heerde, Gupta, and Wittink (2003) in several sentences rather than as a single list citation. The authors use it to interpret their own decomposition results, emphasizing that their smaller brand-switching share is consistent with the cited paper's approximately 33 percent finding and contrasting this with older high-switching results. They also cite it when clarifying the link between elasticity decomposition and unit decomposition. This is a meaningful framing and interpretation role, though not a direct extension of the cited model.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited paper as a substantive empirical benchmark for decomposing promotion effects, especially the size of brand-switching versus primary-demand components. It leverages that comparison to position and interpret its own decomposition findings as aligned with the more recent lower-switching view.
